title: Gestational hypertension: nutrition and monitoring
excerpt: Why blood pressure matters in pregnancy, general eating patterns, home monitoring, and emergency symptoms — not a treatment plan.
content:
## Why it matters

High blood pressure in pregnancy can progress to preeclampsia. Diet alone does not cure hypertension but supports overall health.

## Eating suggestions

- Balanced meals: vegetables, fruits, lean protein, whole grains.
- Moderate sodium unless your clinician prescribes strict limits.
- Adequate calcium and protein as normally recommended.

## Monitoring

- Attend all prenatal visits; home BP monitoring if advised.
- Report headache, vision changes, upper abdominal pain, sudden swelling.

## Emergency

Severe headache, shortness of breath, or BP readings in the danger zone your clinician defined — go to hospital immediately.
